title: auganthus sieboldii
intro: Auganthus sieboldii, also known as Chinese angelica or Dong quai, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and estrogenic effects, making it a popular remedy for menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and other female health issues. The health benefits of Auganthus sieboldii include reducing menstrual pain, improving circulation, and alleviating symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, as well as possessing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of the plant are often used to make teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to support women's health and well-being.
